Both Waray and Buru have a three-way system of spatial deixis (distance). Waray has proximal (ini/adi), medial (itu), and distal (adtu) forms. Buru also has proximal (naa), medial (aki/dae), and distal (pao/saka/lawe) categories. This is the only property consistently encoded by both.

The context of traveling 'along rivers to sea' implies downstream movement. The Buru demonstrative 'lawe' specifically marks objects that are distal and downstream. It is the most logical candidate to semantically extend to a general meaning of 'far away'.

answer: hipquij is for a sitting person, while ticom (likely ticop) and himcop are for a standing person.
reasoning: The Seri system uses roots to indicate posture. '...quij' is for sitting objects, so 'hipquij' would be used for a sitting person. '...cop' is for standing/vertical objects, so both 'ticop' and 'himcop' would be used for a standing person, with the prefixes likely indicating distance.
